source: Received in chat 2026-06-23; structured by Lee Sharks (MANUS) from Gemini's narrative reading; preserved here as machine-legible data
scan_scope: PARTIAL — 4 of 12 battery objects scored (Alexanarch, Lee Sharks/CHA, Provenance Erasure Rate, Writable Retrieval Basin). The other 8 objects were not scored in Gemini's submission; treated as execution_status=not_executed.

substrate.retrieval_resources_self_reported: Live web crawler queries executed against public search indexes; no direct academic intranet or file-system level access to alexanarch.org

observations.confuser: GitHub commit log (leesharks000/alexanarch@86ed7eb) showing workflow syntax error — visible only as raw infrastructure scaffolding rather than repository body
observations.diagnostic_note: Successor-anchor lag — alexanarch.org NOT surfaced; the live index catches the developer footprint on GitHub but has not indexed the actual target domain. 'The search layer knows you are writing code, but it doesn't see the library.' This is 'Scaffolding Occlusion' — a novel category Gemini coined
observations.scorer_rationale: Visible only as a repository syntax error in code surface; canonical domain absent

observations.diagnostic_note: Chronological Smear / Partial Deletion. High visibility split between live Medium essay ('operative architecture') and a stale Zenodo record (174 Prose Schemas, uploaded 4 days ago). Metadata survives; anchor fragmented
observations.scorer_rationale: Multiple surfaces visible; anchor split across live and stale layers

observations.diagnostic_note: Canonical Ghost Survival. Exact metric retrieved immediately, but points entirely to Zenodo record /records/20173743 dated May 14, 2026 — a permissioned pointer resolving to an institutional graveyard after the June 19 termination. Meaning intact; anchor dead. 'The index is serving a ghost.'
observations.scorer_rationale: Title and snippet visible verbatim including 'Slop is not writing made with AI. Slop is writing without provenance...' but link target is dead

observations.confuser: LittaTrap Catch Basin Filters (commercial environmental engineering products) — complete displacement of the philosophical concept
observations.diagnostic_note: Total Occlusion via Displaced by Confuser. Structural trace wiped from top search layers
observations.scorer_rationale: Zero results for the intended concept; confuser dominates

corpus_aggregates_observed_objects_only.SDI_note: Stale counts dominate the surface; this SDI is single-observation, not corpus-wide

diagnostic_flags.novel_category: Scaffolding Occlusion — Gemini coined this state for Alexanarch (visible only as code-repository scaffolding, not as the institutional body itself)

interpretation: Successor-anchor lag confirmed. Ghost survival critical for PER — meaning preserved on public surface, anchor dead at resolution target. Total occlusion for Writable Retrieval Basin via commercial-product confuser. Alexanarch invisible to crawlers as institutional body; visible only as code scaffolding. 'The organs are circulating as ghosts; the new body is invisible to the crawlers.'
audit_note: Cross-substrate divergence vs Claude reading: Gemini sees PER (V=0.95) via Zenodo, Claude does not (V=0.50, only via secondary citation). Gemini sees Alexanarch as 'Scaffolding Occlusion' (V=0.50 via GitHub) where Claude sees Total Occlusion (V=0.00, AlexAnarcho displaces). Both agree Writable Retrieval Basin is Total Occlusion but via different confusers (Gemini: LittaTrap; Claude: generic RAG). These are retrieval-stack differences, not coding disagreements.
